content.title: REIKI
content.summary: {'A': '灵气（Reiki）是一种早在20世纪初在日本引入的精神修行方法，至今仍被其追随者用于治疗身体、情绪和心理不平衡及由此导致的疾病，其原则涉及从业者所使用的技术，他们声称这些技术可以通过被治疗者的身体传递治愈能量，并主张这些技术也可用于自我疗愈。', 'B': '根据灵气哲学，只有通过灵气大师进行的调谐过程，个体才能够接触并引导这种积极能量，一旦建立了这种能力，就被认为是持久的。调谐后，个体被认为有能力让能量流向身体的虚弱或疾病区域，从而激活自然的愈合过程。灵气能量被认为是“智能能量”，它会自动流向这些区域；因此，从业者认为在事先进行特定问题的诊断是不必要的，并且这种实践可以作为预防医学和在出现明显症状之前促进愈合。由于灵气治疗引发的愈合完全是自然的，许多从业者相信它可以与任何其他类型的治疗同时使用而不会产生不良影响；然而，其他人建议，由于患者可能在某些疾病（例如糖尿病）中经历重大的内部改善，因此需要进行仔细监测，因为这种改善可能需要调整药物需求。', 'C': '这段话主要讲了全身Reiki治疗的过程和效果，包括治疗时间、治疗方式、治疗感受等。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了能量治疗的能量源自宇宙本身，不是从治疗者传递给患者的个人能量，因此被认为是无穷无尽的，且不会损害治疗者的个人福祉。', 'E': '这段话主要讲了关于Reiki的争议，一些人反对Reiki，认为它只能提供一种假象中的健康改善，因此只有“安慰剂”效应；虽然Reiki本身并不被认为具有潜在的危害，但一些医疗从业者担心患者可能会过高估计其益处，从而忽视或放弃传统治疗；其他人则反对Reiki的可靠性，因为缺乏对从业者的监管，认为患者可能会受到缺乏知识和技能的非法治疗师的伤害；虽然Reiki与任何特定的宗教教义无关，但一些宗教领袖出于精神原因反对这种实践；然而，其他人认为治疗中涉及的冥想原则增强了他们探索和接纳自己特定宗教的能力。\n\n总结：这段话主要讲了关于Reiki的争议，包括其效果是否只是一种假象、患者是否会忽视传统治疗、从业者是否缺乏监管、宗教领袖对其的反对以及冥想原则对个人宗教信仰的影响。', 'F': '有限的科学研究表明，Reiki的真实性尚未得到证实，但在一项由南格拉斯哥大学医院神经学研究所进行的研究中观察到，接受30分钟Reiki治疗的受试者的心率和血压明显下降，而接受30分钟安慰剂治疗的对照组则没有这种效果，但由于研究对象只有45人，因此研究建议需要进一步研究。另外，在美国进行的一项类似的初步研究中，对患有轻度痴呆的患者进行Reiki治疗似乎对他们的记忆能力产生了积极影响，但研究限制包括对安慰剂效应的不充分分析。', 'G': '这段话主要讲了其他研究试图确定灵气治疗与癌症和中风患者病情改善之间的相关性，其中对于第一种情况的调查显示对疲劳、疼痛和压力程度有一种看似积极的影响，而对于第二个项目则未能揭示治疗与受试者病情和康复的改善之间的联系。有人提出了能量治疗（如灵气）的好处可能在科学上归因于电磁场的影响，然而，大多数研究人员都认为需要进行更广泛的调查。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The spiritual practice of Reiki was first introduced in early 20th century in Japan and continues to be used by its followers today with the intention of treating physical, emotional and mental imbalances and consequent ill-health. The principles of Reiki involve techniques employed by practitioners they say will channel healing energy through the subject's body, and advocates hold that these techniques can also be used for self-healing. The name of the practice itself stems from two Japanese characters, pronounced 'rei' which translates to'unseen' or 'spiritual' and 'ki' meaning 'life force' or 'energy'.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: According to Reiki philosophy, only by undergoing an attunement process performed by a Reiki Master is an individual able to access, then channel this positive energy within, this ability once established is considered to be enduring. Once attuned, it is said that an individual has the ability to allow energy to flow to weak or diseased areas of the body, so activating a natural healing process. Reiki energy is considered to be 'intelligent energy' in that it automatically flows to such areas; for this reason, practitioners believe that diagnosis of a specific problem is unnecessary beforehand and that the practice can be used as preventative medicine and encourage healing prior to the onset of tangible symptoms. Since healing initiated by Reiki treatment is entirely natural, many practitioners are confident that it can be used alongside any other type of treatment without adverse affect; however, others recommend that since the patient may undergo significant internal improvement for certain ailments – diabetes, for example – careful monitoring is required since such improvements may establish a need for an alteration in medication requirements.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: A 'whole body' Reiki treatment session typically lasts between to 90 minutes. The subject is required to lie down – often on a treatment table – clothed in comfortable and loose fitting attire. Treatment may involve the practitioner placing their hands on the recipient in a variety of positions; however, some therapists take a non-touching approach, holding their hands a few centimeters away from the body. Hands are usually held in one position for up to 5 minutes before moving on to the next part of the body; between 12 and 20 hand positions are generally used. Those who have undergone a Reiki treatment session often state that they experienced a pleasant warmness in the area of focus and a feeling of contentment and relaxation throughout the session.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The healing energy is said to originate in the universe itself and is not the passing of personal energy from practitioner to patient; it is therefore thought to be inexhaustible and the personal well-being of the practitioner uncompromised. While some masters and teachers hold that subjects must be receptive to the concept in order for energy to flow, others believe that the attitude of the patient is of no consequence and that benefits will follow regardless; for this reason, those following the latter school of thought say that since Reiki requires no conscious belief it can also benefit the well-being of animals and plant life.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Controversy surrounds the practice of Reiki, some in opposition as they say that Reiki may offer only a perceived improvement in health and therefore only a 'placebo' effect. Whilst the practice of Reiki itself is not necessarily considered potentially harmful, some medical practitioners are concerned that its benefits may be over-estimated by patients and that, as a result, they' may ignore or bandon conventional treatments. Others argue against the reliability of Reiki due to the lack of regulation of practitioners, holding that patients may be left vulnerable to illegitimate therapists who lack knowledge and skill. While Reiki is not connected to any particular religious doctrine, some religious leaders oppose the practice for spiritual reasons; however, others hold that the meditative principles involved in treatment have enhanced their own ability to explore and embrace their own particular religion.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Limited scientific studies in the authenticity of Reiki have been conducted. During research conducted by the Institute of Neurological Studies at South Glasgow University Hospital it was observed that there was a significant decrease in heart rate and blood pressure amongst subjects receiving 30 minutes of Reiki treatment as opposed to a group receiving placebo treatment of 30 minutes rest. Since the test group consisted of a small number of subjects just 45 – the research recommendations concluded a requirement for further studies. A similarly small preliminary study into the potential effects of Reiki on patients suffering mild dementia, conducted in the USA, tentatively suggested that treatment had a positive effect on the subjects' memory abilities; however, research limitations included insufficient analysis of potential placebo affects.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Other studies have also attempted to determine correlation between Reiki treatment and improvement in cancer and stroke patients. Whilst investigations into the first condition indicated a seemingly positive effect on degrees of fatigue, pain and stress experienced by sufferers, the second project failed to reveal a link between treatment and improvement in the subjects' condition and rehabilitation. Theories have been put forward that the benefits of energy treatments such as Reiki may be scientifically attributed to the effect of electromagnetic fields; however, the majority researchers agree that more extensive investigation is required.

collect_option.title: Which THREE of the following statements are true of Reiki?

list.text: Principles for self-healing differ to those used on others.
Attunement is said to have a permanent effect on the recipient.
Its preventative properties are more significant than cure.
There are differences in opinion regarding its use with other therapies.
The treatment typically involves contact between the therapist and the patient.
The recipient's own energy is the key to the philosophy.
Some therapists believe a pessimistic approach affects results.
It is only practiced on human subjects.

questions.analysis:
要快速得到这个答案，我们可以按照以下步骤进行定位和筛选：

1. 首先，我们注意到题干中的关键词是"Reiki"，我们需要在原文中找到与Reiki相关的信息。

2. 在原文中，我们可以找到与Reiki相关的句子："Controversy surrounds the practice of Reiki, some in opposition as they say that Reiki may offer only a perceived improvement in health and therefore only a ‘placebo’ effect." 这句话提到了对Reiki的争议。

3. 接下来，我们需要找到与题干中的三个陈述相关的信息。

 a. "Attunement is said to have a permanent effect on the recipient." 这个陈述提到了调谐对接受者有永久的影响。在原文中，我们可以找到与之相关的句子："Whilst the practice of Reiki itself is not necessarily considered potentially harmful, some medical practitioners are concerned that its benefits may be over-estimated by patients and that, as a result, they may ignore or abandon conventional treatments." 这句话暗示了Reiki的效果可能被过高估计。

 b. "There are differences in opinion regarding its use with other therapies." 这个陈述提到了关于Reiki与其他疗法的使用存在不同意见。在原文中，我们可以找到与之相关的句子："Others argue against the reliability of Reiki due to the lack of regulation of practitioners, holding that patients may be left vulnerable to illegitimate therapists who lack knowledge and skill." 这句话暗示了对于Reiki与其他疗法的使用存在争议。

 c. "Some therapists believe a pessimistic approach affects results." 这个陈述提到了一些治疗师认为悲观的态度会影响结果。在原文中，我们可以找到与之相关的句子："Whilst the practice of Reiki itself is not necessarily considered potentially harmful, some medical practitioners are concerned that its benefits may be over-estimated by patients and that, as a result, they may ignore or abandon conventional treatments." 这句话暗示了一些医疗从业者对于Reiki的效果持悲观态度。

综上所述，根据原文中的相关信息，我们可以得出以下答案：

- Attunement is said to have a permanent effect on the recipient.
- There are differences in opinion regarding its use with other therapies.
- Some therapists believe a pessimistic approach affects results.

questions.title: An overview of the practicalities of how Reiki is performed.
questions.analysis:
题干要求我们找到关于Reiki如何进行的实际操作的概述。在原文中，我们可以找到以下相关信息：

1. A ‘whole body’ Reiki treatment session typically lasts between to 90 minutes.（一次“全身”Reiki治疗通常持续90分钟左右。）

这句话提到了Reiki治疗的时间长度。

2. Treatment may involve the practitioner placing their hands on the recipient in a variety of positions; however, some therapists take a non-touching approach, holding their hands a few centimeters away from the body.（治疗可能涉及治疗师在接受者身上采取各种姿势放置他们的手; 然而，一些治疗师采取非接触的方法，将他们的手放在离身体几厘米的地方。）

这句话提到了治疗师如何在接受者身上放置他们的手。

3. The subject is required to lie down – often on a treatment table – clothed in comfortable and loose fitting attire.（受治疗者需要躺下 - 通常是在治疗台上 - 穿着舒适宽松的衣服。）

这句话提到了受治疗者需要躺下并穿着舒适宽松的衣服。

通过对比题干和原文中的信息，我们可以得出结论，本题对应的段落是C段落。

content.title: Natural pesticide in India
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了一个关于印度棉农的戏剧性故事，展示了农药对人类和环境的破坏性，以及当今农业为何如此依赖农药；同时也展示了在不损失庄稼的情况下停止使用化学农药的可能性，并解释了如何做到。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了印度安得拉邦农民种植棉花的故事，他们因为贫穷、文化水平低以及缺乏农药使用经验，被迫依赖当地小规模农业经销商提供的农药和化肥，并受到跨国化学公司的推广信息的影响。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了棉花农民在开始种植棉花时收益高、农药费用低，但随着时间推移，棉花害虫的进入导致农药抗性增加，农民不得不频繁使用农药来控制害虫，最终导致生态平衡被破坏。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了一个村民决定尝试使用自然方法保护棉花作物的故事。', 'E': '总结：这段话主要讲了印度常见的苦楝树是NPM工具包中最重要的资源，它通过产生多种天然杀虫剂来保护自己免受昆虫侵害。', 'F': '印度传统上使用苦楝树来保护储存的谷物免受昆虫侵害，并生产肥皂、护肤乳液和其他健康产品；苦楝种子经过研磨成粉末，浸泡在水中过夜后喷洒在庄稼上，还可以将苦楝饼混入土壤中以杀死土壤中的害虫和病害，并作为富含氮的有机肥料，苦楝树在当地生长，所以唯一的“成本”是准备苦楝用于田地的劳动力。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了农民通过使用NPM（自然农业管理）取得了成功，不仅收获好于使用农药的农民，而且因为没有花费一分钱购买农药而赚得更多；随后，受到这个成功的启发，20名农民在接下来的一年也尝试了NPM，并且村里的妇女们施加压力要求丈夫停止使用有毒化学物质；不再接触农药的家庭开始感觉更好，收入、健康和整体福祉的快速改善迅速让每个人都认识到NPM的价值；到2000年，Punukula的所有农民都开始使用NPM，不仅用于棉花，还用于其他作物。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了自杀潮结束后，村民们恢复了现金、健康和精力，开始了更多的社区和商业项目，同时妇女们通过收集、研磨和销售印楝种子为其他村庄提供了新的收入来源，村民们还救助了被奴役的孩子并给予他们特殊的六个月“补习”课程以返回学校。', 'I': '通过与农药作斗争并取得胜利，村庄的团结、自信和对未来的乐观情绪得到了增强；当经销商试图通过支付更少的价格来惩罚使用NPM棉花的农民时，农民们团结起来组成了一个营销合作社，在其他地方找到了更公平的价格；普努库拉市民在NPM斗争中培养的领导和合作技能帮助他们应对其他挑战，如水净化、建立棉花轧花机以增加棉花的价值，并说服国家政府支持NPM，而不是跨国农药公司的反对。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: A dramatic story about cotton farmers in India shows how destructive pesticides can be for people and the environment; and why today's agriculture is so dependent on pesticides. This story also shows that it's possible to stop using chemical pesticides without losing a crop to ravaging insects, and it explains how to do it.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The story began about 30 years ago, a handful of families migrated from the Guntur district of Andhra Pradesh, southeast India, into Punukula, a community of around 900 people farming plots of between two and 10 acres. The outsiders from Guntur brought cotton-culture with them. Cotton wooed farmers by promising to bring in more hard cash than the mixed crops they were already growing to eat and sell: millet, sorghum, groundnuts, pigeon peas, mung beans, chili and rice. But raising cotton meant using pesticides and fertilizers – until then a mystery to the mostly illiterate farmers of the community. When cotton production started spreading through Andhra Pradesh state. The high value of cotton made it an exceptionally attractive crop, but growing cotton required chemical fertilizers and pesticides. As most of the farmers were poor, illiterate, and without previous experience using agricultural chemicals, they were forced to rely on local, small-scale agricultural dealers for advice. The dealers sold them seeds, fertilizers, and pesticides on credit and also guaranteed the purchase of their crop. The dealers themselves had little technical knowledge about pesticides. They merely passed on promotional information from multinational chemical companies that supplied their products.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: At first, cotton yields were high, and expenses for pesticides were low because cotton pests had not yet moved in. The farmers had never earned so much! But within a few years, cotton pests like bollworms and aphids plagued the fields, and the farmers saw how rapid insect evolution can be. Repeated spraying killed off the weaker pests, but left the ones most resistant to pesticides to multiply. As pesticide resistance mounted, the farmers had to apply more and more of the pesticides to get the same results. At the same time, the pesticides killed off birds, wasps, beetles, spiders, and other predators that had once provided natural control of pest insects. Without these predators, the pests could destroy the entire crop if pesticides were not used. Eventually, farmers were mixing sometimes having to spray their cotton as frequently as two times a week. They were really hooked!

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text:
The villagers were hesitant, but one of Punukula's village elders decided to risk trying the natural methods instead of pesticides. His son had collapsed with acute pesticide poisoning and survived but the hospital bill was staggering. SECURE's staff coached this villager on how to protect his cotton crop by using a toolkit of natural methods chat India's Center for
Sustainable Agriculture put together in collaboration with scientists at Andhra Pradesh's state university. They called the toolkit "Non-Pesticide Management" – or "NPM."

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The most important resource in the NPM toolkit was the neem tree (Azadirachta indica) which is common throughout much of India. Neem tree is a broad-leaved evergreen tree related to mahogany. It protects itself against insects by producing a multitude of natural pesticides that work in a variety of ways: with an arsenal of chemical defenses that repel egg-laying, interfere with insect growth, and most important, disrupt the ability of crop-eating insects to sense their food.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In fact, neem has been used traditionally in India to protect stored grains from insects and to produce soaps, skin lotions, and other health products. To protect crops from insects, neem seeds are simply ground into a powder that is soaked overnight in water. The solution is then sprayed onto the crop. Another preparation, neem cake, can be mixed into the soil to kill pests and diseases in the soil, and it doubles as an organic fertilizer high in nitrogen. Neem trees grow locally, so the only "cost" is the labor to prepare neem for application to fields.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The first farmer's trial with NPM was a complete success! His harvest was as good as the harvests of farmers that were using pesticides, and he earned much more because he did not spend a single rupee on pesticides. Inspired by this success, 20 farmers tried NPM the next year. SECURE posted two well-trained staff in Punukula to teach and help everyone in the village, and the village women put pressure on their husbands to stop using toxic chemicals. Families that were no longer exposing themselves to pesticides began to feel much better, and the rapid improvement in income, health, and general wellbeing quickly sold everyone on the value of NPM. By 2000, all the farmers in Punukula were using NPM, not only for cotton but for their other crops as well.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The suicide epidemic came to an end. And with the cash, health, and energy that returned when they stopped poisoning themselves with pesticides, the villagers were inspired to start more community and business projects. The women of Punukula created a new source of income by collecting, grinding, and selling neem seeds for NPM in other villages. The villagers rescued their indentured children and gave them special six-month "catch-up," courses to return to school.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Fighting against pesticides, and winning, increased village solidarity, self-confidence, and optimism about the future. When dealers tried to punish NPM users by paying less for NPM cotton, the farmers united to form a marketing cooperative that found fairer prices elsewhere. The leadership and collaboration skills that the citizens of Punukula developed in the NPM struggle have helped them to take on other challenges, like water purification, building a cotton gin to add value to the cotton before they sell it, and convincing the state government to support NPM over the objection of multi-national pesticide corporations.

content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了在我们生活的时代中，强大的形象、吸引人的口号和不容错过的优惠不断地从各个方面轰炸我们，而这些说服者们都在不断地工作。无论是政治家、超市连锁店、销售人员还是广告商，他们都知道如何向我们销售他们的形象、观念或产品，他们运用最熟练的形象制造者和最好的心理技巧，确保即使是最谨慎的人也会对他们的操纵敞开心扉。', 'B': '超市通过布局和商品摆放位置来最大化利润，迎合了我们富有金钱但时间紧张的生活方式。', 'C': '超市为了让我们在里面停留更长的时间，购买更多商品，会努力营造一个愉快的环境，比如播放音乐、散发香气等。此外，超市的忠诚卡也是一种成功的策略，顾客可能以为是因为忠诚而得到回报，但实际上他们在交换自己的购物习惯信息。忠诚顾客可能因为坚持购买自己喜欢的商品而支付高达30%的额外费用。', 'D': '研究表明，75%的利润来自于仅占顾客总数30%的顾客，因此奖励卡可以用来识别和更好地满足这些“精英”顾客，同时也可以用来使广告更加与个体消费者相关，就像斯皮尔伯格的未来惊悚片《少数派报告》中，汤姆·克鲁斯的角色被个性化互动广告轰炸一样。', 'E': '广告行业通过关注产品的功能或者如何让我们感受到的方式，成功地影响了我们的态度和情感，尤其是情感反应在广告中的作用更为重要。', 'F': '广告商利用我们对安全的需求（保险广告）、归属感（时尚广告中让顾客感觉自己属于某个群体）和自尊心（激励性广告）来吸引我们，而名人通常被用作满足这些需求的快速方式，因为名人代表了成功或者因为他们看起来熟悉，从而让产品看起来“安全”。一项对4,000个广告活动的调查发现，使用名人的广告比没有名人的广告效果提高了10%。幽默也能刺激快速的情感反应。《国际广告学杂志》上的Heiman Chung发现，有趣的广告比直接的广告更容易被记住。将幽默与性暗示结合起来，比如Wonderbra的“Hello Boys”广告，你就会成功了。', 'G': '广告的目的是通过展示人们向往的生活方式来吸引消费者，同时利用行为主义理论来刺激消费者的感官体验，从而增强广告的效果。', 'H': '销售的关键在于通过提高买家对产品或服务的需求感来促成销售，而专业销售人员能够利用基本的心理技巧，将不同的产品在最佳时机销售给适合的客户。', 'I': '好的销售人员会倾听客户的需求，并提供与客户合作以实现他们的目标的服务，从而使客户感觉他们正在接受“咨询”而不是销售推销；同时，销售人员表现出一种态度，认为销售将会成功，而不是使用“如果你购买”的说法，而是使用“当你购买”的说法。', 'J': '这段话主要讲述了销售人员在与客户建立关系时，通过询问兴趣、家庭和生活方式等问题来建立共鸣，并尽可能地与买家的风格相匹配，以提高销售效果。', 'K': '销售人员通过寻找“成交信号”来实现销售目标，而不是直接询问客户是否有兴趣，从而达到预期的销售成功。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
We have long lived in an age where powerful images, catchy soundbites and too-good-to miss offers to bombard us from every quarter. All around us the persuaders are at work.
Occasionally their methods are unsubtle –the planting kiss on a baby's head by a wannabe political leader, or a liquidation sale in a shop that has been "closing down" for well over a year, but generally the persuaders know what they are about and are highly capable. Be they politicians, supermarket chains, salespeople or advertisers, they know exactly what to do to sell us their images, ideas or produce. When it comes to persuasion, these giants rule supreme. They employ the most skilled image-makers and use the best psychological tricks to guarantee that even the most cautious among us are open to manipulation.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: We spend more time in them than we mean to, we buy 75 percent of our food from them and end up with products that we did not realize we wanted. Right from the start, supermarkets have been ahead of the game. For example, when Sainsbury introduced shopping baskets into its 1950s stores, it was a stroke of marketing genius. Now shoppers could browse and pick up items they previously would have ignored. Soon after came trolleys, and just as new roads attract more traffic, the same applied to trolley space. Pro Merlin Stone, IBM Professor of Relationship Marketing at Bristol Business School, says aisles are laid out to maximize profits. Stores pander to our money-rich, time-poor lifestyle. Low turnover products —clothes and electrical goods— are stocked at the back while high—turnover items command position at the front.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Stone believes supermarkets work hard to "stall" us because the more time we spend in them, the more we buy. Thus, great efforts are made to make the environment pleasant. Stores play music to relax us and some even pipe air from the in-store bakery around the shop. In the USA, fake aromas are sometimes used. The smell is both the most evocative and subliminal sense. In experiments, pleasant smells are effective in increasing our spending. A casino that fragranced only half its premise saw profit soar in the aroma—filled areas. The other success story from the supermarkets' perspective is the loyalty card. Punters may assume that they are being rewarded for their fidelity, but all the while they are trading information about their shopping habits. Loyal shoppers could be paying 30% more by sticking to their favourite shops for essential cosmetics.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Research has shown that 75 percent of profit comes from just 30 percent of customers. Ultimately, reward cards could be used to identify and better accommodate these "elite"shoppers. It could also be used to make adverts more relevant to individual consumers – rather like Spielberg's futuristic thriller Minority Report, in which Tom Cruise's character is bombarded with interactive personalized ads. If this sounds far-fetched, the data-gathering revolution has already seen the introduction of radio – frequency identification – away to electronically tag products to see who is buying what, FRID means they can follow the product into people homes.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: No matter how savvy we think we are to their ploys, the ad industry still wins. Adverts focus on what products do or on how they make us feel. Researcher Laurette Dube, in the Journal of Advertising Research, says when attitudes are base on "cognitive foundations" (logical reasoning), advertisers use informative appeals. This works for products with a little emotional draw but high functionality, such as bleach. Where attitude is based on effect (i.e, emotions), ad teams try to tap into our feelings. Researchers at the University of Florida recently concluded that our emotional responses to adverts dominate over "cognition".

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Advertisers play on our need to be safe (commercials for insurance), to belong (make a customer feel they are in the group in fashion ads) and for self – esteem (aspirational adverts). With time and space at a premium, celebrities are often used as a quick way of meeting these needs – either because the celeb epitomizes success or because they seem familiar and so make the product seem "safe". A survey of 4,000 campaigns found ads with celebs were 10 percent more effective than without. Humor also stimulates a rapid emotional response. Heiman Chung, writing in the International Journal of Advertising, found that funny ads were remembered for longer than straight ones. Combine humor with sexual imagery – as in Wonderbra's "Hello Boys" ads— and you are on to a winner.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Slice-of-life ads are another tried and tested method—they paint a picture of life as you would like it, but still, one that feels familiar. Abhilasha Mehta, in the Journal of Advertising Research, noted that the more one's self-image tallies with the brand being advertised, the stronger the commercial. Ad makers also use behaviorist theories, recognizing that the more sensation we receive from an object, the better we know it. If an advert for a chocolate bar fails to cause salivation, it has probably failed. No wonder advertisements have been dubbed the "nervous system of the business world".

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Probably all of us could make a sale if the product was something we truly believed in, but professional salespeople are in a different league—the best of them can always sell different items to suitable customers in the best time. They do this by using very basic psychological techniques. Stripped to its simplest level, selling works by heightening the buyer's perception of how much they need a product or service. Buyers normally have certain requirements by which they will judge the suitability of a product. The seller, therefore, attempts to tease out what these conditions are and then explains how their products' benefit can meet these requirements.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Richard Hession, author of Be a Great Salesperson says it is human nature to prefer to speak rather listen, and good salespeople pander to this. They ask punters about their needs and offer to work with them to achieve their objectives. As a result, the buyer feels they are receiving a"consultation" rather than a sales pitch. All the while, the salesperson presents with a demeanour that takes it for granted that the sale will be made. Never will the words "if you buy" be used, but rather "when you buy".

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Dr Rob Yeung, a senior consultant at business psychologists Kiddy and Partner, says most salespeople will build up a level of rapport by asking questions about hobbies, family and lifestyle. This has the double benefit of making the salesperson likeable while furnishing him or her with more information about the client's wants. Yeung says effective salespeople try as far as possible to match their style of presenting themselves to how the buyer comes across. If the buyer cracks jokes, the salespeople will respond in kind. If the buyer wants detail, the seller provides it, if they are more interested in the feel of the product, the seller will focus on this. At its most extreme, appearing empathetic can even include the salesperson attempting to"mirror" the hobby language of the buyer.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Whatever the method used, all salespeople work towards one aim: "closing the deal". In fact, they will be looking for "closing signals" through their dealings with potential clients. Once again the process works by assuming success. The buyer is not asked "are you interested?" as this can invite a negative response. Instead, the seller takes it for granted that the deal is effectively done: when the salesman asks you for a convenient delivery date or asks what color you want, you will probably respond accordingly. Only afterwards might you wonder why you proved such a pushover.

questions.title: What is the supermarket's purpose of using “basket” in paragraph B?

options.text: Create a convenient atmosphere of supermarket
Make customers spend more time on shopping
Relieve pressure on the supermarket's traffic
More than half items bought need to be carried

questions.analysis:
这道题要求我们找出超市使用“basket”（购物篮）的目的。我们可以通过以下步骤快速得到答案：

1. 首先，我们需要在原文中找到与超市和购物篮相关的信息。在原文中提到了Sainsbury（超市）和shopping baskets（购物篮）。

2. 接下来，我们需要找到关于购物篮的具体信息。原文中提到了Sainsbury在1950年代的店铺引入了购物篮，这被称为“a stroke of marketing genius”（营销的天才之举）。

3. 根据原文，购物篮的引入使得顾客可以浏览并选择之前可能忽略的商品。

4. 因此，购物篮的目的是让顾客在购物时花费更多的时间。

综上所述，正确答案是B. Make customers spend more time on shopping（让顾客在购物时花费更多的时间）。
